TRANSCRIPT
Operation Quickstart, this is the Mental Game, Part 2, which is all about how I keep
my mental game intact.
So, I talked about mental game or your inner game, and how that's the absolute
most important thing for your success as an entrepreneur. It's not what's happening
outside your body, really, outside of your mind. It's not the external factors. It's not what
your competitors are doing, what the market is doing, what the economy is doing. It's
what's happening inside your head, and staying at a high mental state, so that you can
continue to create, that you can continue to overcome, that you can keep moving forward,
that you can keep your momentum, that you can regain your momentum if you lose it,
that you can deal with setbacks. That's what mental game is. And it is the most important
thing, the most important factor in your success, in getting started, and in your
maintaining, getting momentum, maintaining momentum, and then just having the
stamina to keep your business going.
It's been over 20 years for me. And the one thing I feel good about telling you is
that I've got really good stamina. And I can keep on grinding away. And the reason I keep
on grinding away, I can keep on grinding away, is because I work really hard on my
mental game. I work really hard on my inner game. And I built in a lot of things within
my life to keep my mental game, if not at a peak, at least at a very high level. And that's
what this section's about.
So, one of the most important things, and I'm coming up on 10 years where I've
really focused on this, is my morning ritual. So, every morning when I wake up, I have a
ritual. I have these habits that I go through that set me up for a really strong, powerful
day.
So, really the first thing I do is oral hygiene, brushing my teeth and flossing my
teeth, and hydrating, drinking 16 ounces of water. So I mean, it's simple, but right now
there's so much research coming out about the importance of oral hygiene and for your
entire body. And part of your mental game is staying healthy. If you're not healthy, if
you're getting colds, if you're getting sick, if you're getting the flu, you just can't create.
You can't stay on top of your game if you're getting sick.
So, I'm a big fan of oral hygiene, staying hydrated, and then right after that, I go
right into yoga. I do about 15 to 20 minutes very light yoga, nothing too intense. I do it on
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
my own. I have my own home practice. I basically watched some videos and developed a
practice. Took a bunch of classes. I've been to many yoga classes, but then I would just
take that and incorporate it, so that I would do it on my own. And, so there's no friction. I
go straight from my bed to the bathroom, and do the oral hygiene and get hydrated, and
I'm on my mat, my yoga mat, just a few minutes later, 15 to 20 minutes of stretching out,
stretching my body, it's just huge. And over time, the impact is amazing.
Then the next part of my morning ritual is meditation. And I usually do 20 minutes
of meditation in the morning. This is again, this is something I've been doing for, coming
up on 20 years. And, there's a lot ... Boy, this is something you can go really deep on, and I
am not qualified to go really deep on it, but I know you can keep it really simple.
So there's some apps I use. There's one called Headspace. Lots and lots of people
use Headspace. You can get on your phone and it works great. And it takes you from
absolutely beginning, never having meditated before, and walks you through, and you can
stick with it for a long time. Another one, app, I like quite a bit is called, Relax Melodies,
that's Relax Melodies. And it's just a very, very simple app that plays background sounds,
that's got a timer on it, that has binaural beats. It works really great.
So, those are two of my favorites. There's lots and lots of other meditation apps. Or,
you can do it without an app. But I've found that when I put 20 minutes in, it clears my
mind and sets me up for a very productive day. When I meditate in the morning, I always
have a much more productive day. I think that 20 minutes that I spend meditating, it's
like an investment that pays back an extra hour or maybe two hours of productivity in a
day.
And then, another thing I'll do is a little bit of journaling, maybe five minutes or ten
minutes of just freeform writing, whatever's on my mind in the morning. It's all about
clearing your mind, so you can go forward in a really relaxed but focused manner in the
day. So again, there's lots of ways to journal. You can go real deep on it. It's just for me,
it's sit down for five or ten minutes, and just sort of clearing my mind. If I don't know
what to write, then I'll just put the pen on the paper, and I'll start writing. It doesn't really
matter what I write. I'm not trying to create art. I'm not trying to create something I'm
gonna go back and read after, although sometimes I do that. There are other times during
the day where I might journal and sort of write down what's happening in my day. And
maybe that's something I reflect back on. But in the morning, it's just sort of about
clearing your mind.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Another habit that I have is what I call, the three wins. And I learned this from my
coach, Dan Sullivan. And I do this, a lot of times I'll do it when I go to bed, most of time
when I go to bed, sometimes when I wake up. And what I do is I write down three wins I
had that day, and then three wins I'm going to have the next day. Or if I'm doing it in the
morning, it's three wins I had yesterday, and three wins I'm going to have today. And the
wins don't have to be epic. They could be really simple. But the idea is that you are setting
your day up as a win. Put that one in the win column.
So, it could be, I just had a wonderful hug in the morning with my wife. Or it could
be, I had a great meditation session. Or it could be, I had a great dinner. Or it could be, I
created my lead magnet. It could be business. It could be big. It could be small. But no
matter what, you can find three things that went well in any day. There are always three
wins to be found. And so this is just a practice of finding and recognizing your wins.
And then, when you're setting up wins for the next day, when you're writing those
down, they don't have to be epic huge like amazing wins. Keep them simple. Make it so
that you can achieve them. And, by the way, if you don't achieve them, the next night or
the next morning, when you're writing down your three wins, you don't even have to look
back and see what you anticipated your wins were gonna be. It doesn't matter. Write
down three wins that you had. And if you wanna write four or five, that's fine as well.
So in other words, I go to bed tonight, I write down three wins I had or three wins
I'm gonna have tomorrow. Then tomorrow, even if I don't have those three wins, if I have
three other wins, it doesn't matter. I just write them down. And this is a really powerful
practice. And if you start doing this every day, you'll set yourself up to just find the
positive. And that could be super, super powerful. This doesn't mean you're sticking your
head in the sand, and you're all Pollyanna, and everything's perfect. It's just finding the
wins that you had. And then a lot of times I'm writing those down, I'll write down two or
three things that I'm grateful for, two or three things that I have gratitude for. And of
gratitude practices immensely powerful.
Now, another thing I do is regular body work, meaning I get regular massages,
Rolfing, I do float sessions in sensory deprivation tanks. Sometimes, I've done sound
therapy. I've done a little bit of acupuncture. But these are the things that keep my body
tuned. Chiropractor or chiropractic. These are all things that just keep my body in
alignment, and keep my body feeling good. And just taking care of your shell, of the shell
of your body, contributes in a big way to taking care of your inner game and your brain.
And so, I do these regularly. Very, very powerful.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
And related is regular exercise. So, I try to get exercise, I mean ideally every day. If
not every day, several times a week. And I love to get cardio exercise. So for me, it's
running, it's cycling, road biking, and mountain biking. I play a lot of tennis, like
competitive tennis. Sometimes, I go to the gym, do some strength training. I get out for
hikes in the woods. So those are mine. I mean, you can have yours. It could be swimming.
It could be basketball. It could be some type of martial arts, doesn't matter, but exercise is
incredibly powerful for your brain.
And then another one that's very related is getting outside. I am blessed to live in
an area where I'm surrounded by the mountains, and the woods, and rivers. And so, I like
to get out for walks in the woods. And I do that several times a week, I'll just be going
outside for a walk in the woods. And I also program into my year that I do wilderness
trips, where I get out in the wilderness, longer wilderness trips, often two or three week-
long wilderness trips, where I'm completely disconnected, where I've got no phone, no
Internet. And in terms of long term recharge, those trips are just incredibly, incredibly
powerful for me.
And then, another big one for me, is that I try ... I'm in a situation where I can
completely control my own destiny, my own schedule, and so I try to regularly get out to
masterminds and meetings with other like-minded people, with other entrepreneurs. So,
I try to do that at least every other month. I'll put myself in some type of a situation where
I'm around a lot of other positive entrepreneurs who can just get me pumped up, and I
can get them pumped up. This isn't about motivation, but just when you're around other
creators, it is just naturally lifts you up. And so, I try to do that six times a year, get
around people that will pump me up.
So, that's how I keep on top of my mental game. And, I know the difference
between being at a high state in your mental game, and being at a low state, it's amazing.
It's really hard to create when you're not in a good frame of mind, when you're not in a
good mental state. So, your practices might be different than mine, but I've developed
these over the last 20 years, and they work really well for me. And I know lots and lots of
other entrepreneurs, and the ones that are really on top of their mental game, they usually
use a lot of similar practices, and habits, and tools, as what I just went over.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
